div1.entryFree: Session, the sitting of a council, particularly of a court of justice: when to the --s of sweet silent thought I summon up remembrance of things past, Sonn. 30, 1. from this s. interdict every fowl of tyrant wing, Phoen. 9. summon a s. that we may arraign our most disloyal lady, Wint. II, 3, 202. every shop, church, s., hanging, yields a careful man work, IV, 4, 702. H8 II, 4, 66. Oth. I, 2, 86. III, 3, 140 (Ff --s). to hold s.: Meas. V, 376. Lr. V, 3, 54. --s, in the same sense: this --s even pushes gainst our heart, Wint. III, 2, 1. the --s shall proceed, Wint. III, 2, 1 in --s sit, Oth. III, 3, 140 (Qq s.). cf. Sonn. 30, 1.
